validation.stdout_sample:
usage: compute_gc_content [-h] [--sequence SEQUENCE] [--input INPUT]
                          [--format {raw,fasta}] [--strict]
                          [--ignore-ambiguous] [--output {json,text}]

Compute GC content (fraction of G/C bases) for one or more DNA sequences.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --sequence SEQUENCE   DNA sequence provided directly on the command line.
                        Can be repeated.
  --input INPUT         Read sequences from a file path, or '-' to read from
                        stdin. Supports raw or FASTA based on --format.
  --format {raw,fasta}  Input format: raw or fasta.
  --strict              If set, error on invalid characters (anything other
                        than A/C/G/T/N, ignoring whitespace).
  --ignore-ambiguous    If set, compute GC fraction over only A/C/G/T bases
                        (exclude N/ambiguous from denominator).
  --output {json,text}  Output format: json or text.

usage: find_connected_components [-h] [--edges-json EDGES_JSON]
                                 [--edges-file EDGES_FILE] [--edge EDGE]
                                 [--include-isolated] [--node NODE] [--sort]
                                 [--output {json,text}]

Compute connected components in an undirected graph from an edge list.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --edges-json EDGES_JSON
                        Edges as JSON string or '@path'. Formats: [["a","b"],
                        ...] or [{"left":"a","right":"b"}, ...].
  --edges-file EDGES_FILE
                        Path to a JSON file containing edges. Same formats as
                        --edges-json.
  --edge EDGE           Add one edge as 'LEFT,RIGHT'. Can be provided multiple
                        times.
  --include-isolated    If set, include isolated nodes (nodes with no edges)
                        as singleton components. Provide nodes via --node.
  --node NODE           Declare a node (useful for isolated nodes). Can be
                        provided multiple times.
  --sort                Sort nodes within components and sort components by
                        (size desc, lexicographic) for stable output.
  --output {json,text}  Output format: 'json' or 'text'. Default: json.

usage: reverse_complement_dna.py [-h] --sequence SEQUENCE
                                 [--output {json,text}]

Return the reverse complement of a DNA sequence.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --sequence SEQUENCE   str value for sequence.
  --output {json,text}  Output format.

usage: summarize_numeric_columns.py [-h] --rows-json ROWS
                                    [--output {json,text}]

Compute min, max, and mean for numeric columns in row dictionaries.

options:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  --rows-json ROWS      JSON value for rows.
  --output {json,text}  Output format.
